family_name: Closed-neighborhood balanced colorings of quintic circulants
strongest_honest_claim: As of 2026-04-12T03:20:51-0400, the strongest honest publication-facing claim is still a Route I theorem-slice candidate: for G = C_n(a,b,m) with n = 2m and 1 <= a < b < m, if sigma_{n,a,b}(k) = 1 + 2 cos(2*pi*a*k/n) + 2 cos(2*pi*b*k/n) + (-1)^k is nonzero at every discrete frequency, then G is not CNBC. The CNB-to-kernel bridge `kernel_witness_of_intendedStatement`, the shell `routeI_spectral_obstruction_skeleton`, and the closure theorem `routeI_of_trivialKernel` are now preserved in both `artifacts/families/cnbc_quintic_nonexistence/lean/AutoMath/Families/CNBCQuinticRouteI.lean` and `lean/AutoMath/Families/CNBCQuinticRouteI.lean`, and both copies were checked with direct local `lean`. However, the family Fourier diagonalization and the reusable implication sigma nowhere zero -> TrivialQuinticKernel are still missing, so the honest publication label remains SLICE_CANDIDATE rather than SLICE_EXACT.
paper_title_hint: A Spectral Obstruction to Closed-Neighborhood Balanced Colorings of Quintic Circulants
theorem_slice_target: Route I only: prove that for G = C_n(a,b,m) with n = 2m and 1 <= a < b < m, if the six-term closed-neighborhood symbol sigma_{n,a,b}(k) is nonzero at every discrete frequency, then G is not CNBC.
fallback_target: If the Route I theorem slice still does not close as a referee-facing theorem, preserve it as a method-and-boundaries package: keep the CNB-to-kernel translation and the mirrored routeI_of_trivialKernel shell, use c16-4-5-8-cnbc as the lead direct corollary, keep c20-2-3-10-cnbc as structurally compatible support, and present c24-4-5-12-cnbc plus the alternating boundary on C_10(2,3,5) as explicit theorem limits.
next_blocker: The live blocker is now only the missing family Fourier diagonalization of the six-term circulant operator B_{n,a,b} together with the reusable lemma sigma_{n,a,b}(k) != 0 for all k -> TrivialQuinticKernel n a b m. The official backend copy at lean/AutoMath/Families/CNBCQuinticRouteI.lean has been restored, so the remaining gap is mathematical rather than synchronization-only.
next_action: Keep the campaign fixed on Route I only. Work inside lean/AutoMath/Families/CNBCQuinticRouteI.lean and formalize the family Fourier diagonalization of B_{n,a,b} together with the implication sigma nowhere zero -> TrivialQuinticKernel. Only after that closes should c16-4-5-8-cnbc be packaged as the lead direct corollary; keep c20-2-3-10-cnbc as support and c24-4-5-12-cnbc plus C_10(2,3,5) as explicit theorem boundaries unless a reusable Route II lemma is actually written.
